items.title: Machine Learning
items.description: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning. ML covers learning from data broadly; DL specializes in multi-layer neural networks and representation learning when data scale and modality justify it.

items.title: Multilayer Perceptron
items.description: The MLP is the foundational feedforward deep network: stacked affine transforms plus nonlinear activations trained with backpropagation. DL architectures extend this core idea with convolutions, attention, and other layer types.

items.title: Deep Learning Frameworks
items.description: Practitioners build and train deep models in PyTorch, TensorFlow, and similar frameworks that provide tensors, autograd, modules, and GPU acceleration for large-scale representation learning.

items.title: Generative AI
items.description: Modern generative models — transformers, diffusion models, GANs — are deep networks trained to model and sample complex data distributions. Gen AI sits on DL capabilities but adds generation-specific objectives and products.
